title: Same Tree
code: public bool IsSameTree(TreeNode? a, TreeNode? b)
if (a is null && b is null) return true;
if (a is null || b is null) return false;

frames.caption: Compare the two trees by recursing on matching positions: a pair is identical when values are equal and both child pairs are identical.
frames.note: Same-tree is a structural recursion that walks both trees in lockstep. At every position we ask the same question — are these two nodes (and everything below them) the same? — so the trees stay perfectly aligned as we descend.

frames.caption: Both nodes exist. Compare values: 5 == 5 → equal, so recurse into the left then right subtrees.
frames.note: Both nodes exist, so compare their values. If they differ we could return false right here. If they match we have not finished — identity also requires the left subtrees to match AND the right subtrees to match. So we recurse left first, then right, and AND the three results together.

frames.caption: Both nodes exist. Compare values: 3 == 3 → equal, so recurse into the left then right subtrees.
frames.note: Both nodes exist, so compare their values. If they differ we could return false right here. If they match we have not finished — identity also requires the left subtrees to match AND the right subtrees to match. So we recurse left first, then right, and AND the three results together.

frames.caption: Both nodes exist. Compare values: 1 == 1 → equal, so recurse into the left then right subtrees.
frames.note: Both nodes exist, so compare their values. If they differ we could return false right here. If they match we have not finished — identity also requires the left subtrees to match AND the right subtrees to match. So we recurse left first, then right, and AND the three results together.

frames.caption: Both sides are null here — an empty subtree pair is identical → true.
frames.note: Base case one: both pointers are null. Two empty subtrees are trivially identical, so this branch returns true. This is the case that lets a matched pair "bottom out" — the recursion stops descending the moment both sides run out of nodes at the same place.

frames.caption: Both sides are null here — an empty subtree pair is identical → true.
frames.note: Base case one: both pointers are null. Two empty subtrees are trivially identical, so this branch returns true. This is the case that lets a matched pair "bottom out" — the recursion stops descending the moment both sides run out of nodes at the same place.

frames.caption: Value 1 matched and both subtrees matched → this pair is identical.
frames.note: Values are equal and both child recursions came back true, so this entire subtree pair is confirmed identical — color it match. Because the check is value AND left AND right, a single mismatch anywhere underneath would have collapsed this to false. The whole walk is O(min(n, m)): it stops at the smaller tree.

frames.caption: Every node pair matched → the trees are identical → return true.
frames.note: The roots matched and every subtree pair matched, so the two trees are identical → true. Same-tree is the primitive the subtree check reuses: IsSubtree just runs this comparison rooted at each node of the larger tree.
